# Community

> *Community nickname: the PISS (Post-Ideological Society System) — because we're done with ideological pissing contests.*

## What This Community Is

A group of people following or contributing to an open-air philosophical investigation into coordination across genuine difference. We come from different disciplines, different professional contexts, different worldviews and different levels of familiarity with the ideas in this repository. That diversity is a feature, not a problem to be managed.

This is not a finished framework looking for adopters. It's thinking being done in public, currently grounded in doctoral research on whether structural interdependence can formally ground coordination without value convergence. The hope is that the thinking eventually outgrows its current scope. The current scope is intentionally narrow.

## What We Value

**Substance over performance.** We care about the quality of thinking, not the credentials behind it. A sharp critique from a nurse who's seen coordination fail on her ward is worth as much as a published paper from a political theorist. Bring what you know.

**Honesty over comfort.** The work commits to being honest about its own incompleteness. The community must do the same. If something is wrong, say so clearly. If you disagree with a core claim, make the case. Politeness is welcome. Softening a genuine challenge to avoid discomfort is not.

**Engagement over agreement.** You don't need to agree with the author to contribute. Some of the most valuable contributions will come from people who think the whole approach is mistaken, as long as they can articulate why. Coordination across difference starts here.

**Patience over urgency.** This work is being built carefully. There is no ship date. Contributions that push the thinking forward are always welcome. Contributions that demand immediate action or resolution are less useful. Some tensions need to be held, not solved.

## How to Participate

**Watch and star the repository** if you want to follow development.

**Open an issue** — you'll see guided templates for different types of contribution, or a freeform option if you prefer.

**Join a discussion** in the Issues tab — browse existing conversations before starting a new one.

**Contribute content** via pull request or email. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for details.

**Share the work** if you think someone in your network would find it useful. The most useful thing a growing readership can do is connect the project with people and perspectives it hasn't yet reached.

## Who We're Looking For

The current investigation engages philosophy, game theory, decision theory and institutional design. Critique is most useful from people working in or adjacent to:

- **Philosophy and political theory** — to challenge the intellectual foundations, especially on value pluralism, incommensurability and collective action
- **Decision theory and formal epistemology** — to stress-test the formal claims about coordination under severe uncertainty
- **Evolutionary game theory and social choice** — to probe where the proposed structural features actually generate stable coordination
- **Institutional design and public policy** — to test whether the mechanisms could work in practice
- **Defence, security and diplomacy** — to stress-test against high-stakes, multi-party coordination realities where shared payoff structures don't exist
- **Frontline public servants** (healthcare, education, emergency services) — to ground the theory in operational reality
- **Anyone who's experienced coordination across genuine difference** — at any scale, in any context

If the project eventually earns a broader scope, the list of perspectives it should be engaging with will grow accordingly. Right now the request is narrower: help stress-test the specific philosophical question being worked through.

## What This Community Is Not

A political movement. A spiritual community. A think tank. A protest. An ideology.

It's a reading room with the door open. Someone is doing doctoral-track philosophical work and sharing the thinking as it develops. Engagement sharpens the work. The quality of the engagement depends on the quality of the people who show up and their willingness to work across the differences between them.
